title: EvaluateBatchResponse
description: Response from POST /v2/evaluate:batch. items[i] corresponds to the request at requests[i] in the inbound payload — order preservation is a wire guarantee. Each item is a tagged union: permitted=true carries the same fields as v1 EvaluateResponse, permitted=false carries a denial with reason + audit_hash so callers can log denied attempts without a second round-trip.

batch_id.description: UUID identifying the batch. Surfaces as batch_id on every item and as a single hash-chain entry in audit_events listing all contained decision_ids.

items.description: Ordered per-item decisions. Same length as the inbound requests array; items[i] decides requests[i].

index.description: Zero-based position in the inbound requests array. Redundant with array ordering but carried so callers can regroup after parallel post-processing.

permitted.description: True on allow, false on deny/hold/escalate. Hold/escalate surface via the reason field — SDKs translate into higher-level AtlaSentDenied / hold-pending states.

decision_id.description: Per-item permit id. Same semantics as v1 EvaluateResponse.decision_id. Pass to /v1-verify-permit (or /v2/permits/:id/verify) independently of the batch.

reason.description: Human-readable explanation. Always present; empty string if the policy engine has nothing to add on allow.

audit_hash.description: Per-item audit-chain hash. Present even on deny so audit exports carry a complete record.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of this item's decision. Items in the same batch share a batch_id but can have distinct timestamps (the engine evaluates them as a stream).

batch_id.description: Echo of the top-level batch_id, repeated per item so consumers that process items independently still have the correlation.

proof_id.description: Present only when the corresponding request carried a payload_hash (Pillar 9 opt-in). Points to the proof row this decision will be consumed against.

proof_status.description: Pillar 9 proof SLA. 'not_applicable' means the request didn't opt in to the proof flow. 'pending' means evaluate has cleared but consume has not. 'executed' / 'failed' after consume. Callers poll via GET /v2/proofs/:id.
